name: movinet_attention_pool_head

feature_request.exact_feature: Replace the current MoViNet average-pool classifier head with a lightweight learned attention-pooling head that reads the final MoViNet feature map using a dedicated query-style pooling operation, then emits the existing binary violence logits.
feature_request.why_this_variant: The previous MobileViT-style replacement regressed on all six evaluation summary metrics, which indicates that aggressive token compression plus a tiny transformer readout was the wrong replacement for this partially frozen A5 setup. The recommended revision keeps the backbone's late feature geometry intact and changes only the pooling/readout behavior, which is the most direct way to test whether fixed average pooling is the real limitation while staying lightweight and better aligned with pretrained MoViNet features.
production_context_from_user: The user wants the next iteration to remain a true replacement head rather than an additive side branch.
The user believes the current classifier head can be beaten and should not be treated as final just because it was historically chosen.

primary_paper.title: Learning Transferable Visual Models From Natural Language Supervision

supporting_materials.title: TokenLearner: What Can 8 Learned Tokens Do for Images and Videos?
MobileViT: Light-weight, General-purpose, and Mobile-friendly Vision Transformer

constraints: Feature must be optional and config-switchable.
Use the baseline TOML as the default base unless explicitly changed.
Do not modify or replace the pretrained MoViNet backbone.
Keep the task as binary violence classification.
Keep the replacement head lightweight and practical for the existing A5 training setup.
The revision should preserve ranking-sensitive production priorities, especially PPK-style ordering quality under tight false-positive limits.

user_signoff.approval_notes: User approved the movinet_attention_pool_head replacement-head direction in chat on 2026-04-15.
